rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical four-component 1,2-difunctionalization of alkenes with diaryl diselenides, CS2, and secondary amines to give beta-selenyl dithiocarbamates via simultaneous C-Se and C-S bond formation
electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh to a radical cation that cleaves to PhSe radical (II) and PhSe cation (III); anode also oxidizes the in-situ generated dithiocarbamate anion (VI) to dithiocarbamate cation (VII), which couples to disulfide 3g. Cathode reduces PhSe+ back to 1/2 PhSeSePh and reduces protons to H2.
electron_flow: At the anode, PhSeSePh is oxidized (-1e) to a radical cation that fragments into PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds to styrene to give a benzylic C-radical (IV) that is either trapped by 3g (path a) or further anodically oxidized to benzylic cation VIII which is captured by dithiocarbamate anion VI (path b), while PhSe+ alternatively forms seleniranium V that opens with VI (path c). In parallel, CS2 + amine form dithiocarbamate VI, which is anodically oxidized (-2e) to cation VII coupling to give disulfide 3g; the cathode reduces protons to H2 and PhSe+ back to PhSeSePh.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 3.0 equiv TEMPO completely suppressed formation of 4a (0% yield), indicating a radical pathway involving PhSe• and/or benzylic C-radical

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl (1d) without electricity
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric PhSeCl with CS2/amine/K2CO3 and no current gave 4a in 18% yield, supporting involvement of an electrophilic Se / seleniranium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: bis(dithiocarbamoyl) disulfide 3g
probe_reagents.observation: Using preformed 3g instead of CS2 + amine under electrolysis gave 4a in 71% yield, supporting 3g as a key intermediate

probe_reagents.name: sodium diethyldithiocarbamate (3h)
probe_reagents.observation: Using preformed dithiocarbamate salt 3h gave 4a in 42% yield, consistent with dithiocarbamate anion VI as an on-cycle species

notes: Three competing product-forming pathways are proposed: (path a) radical IV + disulfide 3g; (path b) anodic oxidation of IV to benzylic cation VIII trapped by dithiocarbamate VI; (path c) PhSe+ forms seleniranium V which is opened by VI. The authors do not definitively distinguish among them. CV data (Ep = 0.09 and 1.13 V for dithiocarbamate; 1.90 and 1.64 V for PhSeSePh vs Ag/AgCl) support the order of anodic oxidations.
